record.field: PocketBase is an open-source backend in a single file, bundling a database, authentication, file storage, realtime subscriptions and an admin dashboard.

PocketBase is an open-source backend in a single file, bundling a database, authentication, file storage, realtime subscriptions and an admin dashboard.

Written in Go, it ships as one portable executable with an embedded SQLite database and an auto-generated REST API, giving you a complete Firebase-style backend with no external services to wire up. You can run it standalone or embed it as a Go library to add custom business logic, and clients connect through official JavaScript and Dart SDKs. It is a popular choice for developers who want a self-hosted, low-friction backend they fully control.

Key features
  • Embedded SQLite database with schema builder and auto-generated REST API
  • Built-in user authentication, including email/password and OAuth2 providers
  • Realtime subscriptions to record changes over server-sent events
  • File storage with local or S3-compatible backends and on-the-fly thumbnails
  • Web admin dashboard for managing collections, records, users and settings
  • Extensible via JavaScript hooks or as a Go library, packaged as one binary
Best for

Developers, indie hackers and small teams building web, mobile or desktop apps that need a self-hosted backend with auth, storage and realtime data without operating a multi-service stack. Ideal for MVPs, prototypes and small-to-medium production apps where a single, easy-to-deploy binary is preferred.

services: pocketbase: image: adrianmusante/pocketbase:latest restart: always expose: - 8090 volumes: - ./pocketbase_data:/pocketbase environment: - POCKETBASE_ADMIN_EMAIL={{ admin_email }} - POCKETBASE_ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ admin_password }} - POCKETBASE_ADMIN_UPSERT=true - POCKETBASE_PORT_NUMBER=8090 healthcheck: test: - CMD - wget - -qO- - http://localhost:8090/_/ interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8090' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
